title: "7 Free GitHub Repos Every Beginner Should Star Right Now"
content:
> *"GitHub isn't just for hosting your code. It's the biggest free library of developer knowledge on the planet. These 7 repos have helped more developers than any paid course ever will."*
 
Okay real talk. рҹ‘Ү
 
Most beginners use GitHub for one thing вҖ” pushing their own code.
 
That's like having a library card and only using it to store your own diary. рҹҳӮ
 
GitHub has thousands of FREE repos that are basically entire curriculums, roadmaps, cheat sheets, and interview prep guides вҖ” maintained by some of the best developers in the world.
 
And most beginners have NO idea they exist. рҹҳӨ
 
This post changes that.
 
**7 repos. All free. All life-changing for a beginner.** Hit вӯҗ on all of them before you close this tab.
 
Let's go. рҹ‘Ү
 
---
 
## вӯҗ Repo #1: developer-roadmap
### рҹ”— github.com/kamranahmedse/developer-roadmap
 
**Stars:** 300,000+ вӯҗ (one of the most starred repos on GitHub)
**What it is:** Visual roadmaps for every developer path рҹ—әпёҸ
 
This is THE starting point for every developer. Period.
 
Confused about what to learn next? Open this repo. It shows you exactly what skills connect to what, in what order, for every type of developer role.
 
```plaintext
Roadmaps available рҹ—әпёҸ
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вң… Frontend Developer
вң… Backend Developer
вң… Full Stack Developer
вң… React Developer
вң… Node.js Developer
вң… DevOps Engineer
вң… AI/ML Engineer
вң… And 15 more...
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
```
 
**What makes it special:** рҹҺҜ
 
Each roadmap is interactive at roadmap.sh вҖ” click any skill to see resources, explanations, and whether it's required or optional.
 
> рҹ’Ў **How to use it:** Open the Frontend or React roadmap. Mark what you already know. See what's next. That's your learning plan вҖ” free, visual, maintained by the community. рҹҺҜ
 
---
 
## вӯҗ Repo #2: javascript-algorithms
### рҹ”— github.com/trekhleb/javascript-algorithms
 
**Stars:** 187,000+ вӯҗ
**What it is:** Every algorithm and data structure вҖ” in JavaScript рҹ§ 
 
Interview season is coming. This repo has every data structure and algorithm you'll ever be asked about вҖ” implemented in clean JavaScript with explanations.
 
```plaintext
What's inside рҹ“ҡ
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
Data Structures:
  вң… Linked Lists, Trees, Graphs
  вң… Stack, Queue, Heap
  вң… Hash Tables, Tries
 
Algorithms:
  вң… Sorting (bubble, merge, quick, heap...)
  вң… Searching (binary, linear...)
  вң… Dynamic Programming
  вң… Recursion patterns
  вң… String manipulation
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
Each one has:
  вҶ’ Clean JavaScript implementation
  вҶ’ Time + space complexity
  вҶ’ Visual explanation
  вҶ’ Links to learn more
```
 
```javascript
// Example вҖ” Binary Search from the repo рҹ”Қ
function binarySearch(sortedArray, seekElement) {
  let startIndex = 0
  let endIndex = sortedArray.length - 1
 
  while (startIndex <= endIndex) {
    const middleIndex = Math.floor((startIndex + endIndex) / 2)
 
    if (sortedArray[middleIndex] === seekElement) {
      return middleIndex  // found! вң…
    }
 
    if (sortedArray[middleIndex] < seekElement) {
      startIndex = middleIndex + 1  // search right half
    } else {
      endIndex = middleIndex - 1    // search left half
    }
  }
 
  return -1  // not found
}
```
 
> рҹ’Ў **How to use it:** 2 weeks before interviews вҖ” read through the data structures section. Don't memorize. Understand. The patterns repeat. рҹҺҜ
 
---
 
## вӯҗ Repo #3: You-Dont-Know-JS
### рҹ”— github.com/getify/You-Dont-Know-JS
 
**Stars:** 179,000+ вӯҗ
**What it is:** The deepest free JavaScript book series ever written рҹ“–
 
Kyle Simpson wrote 6 complete books about JavaScript. Published them FREE on GitHub. Forever.
 
These books explain the parts of JavaScript that most tutorials skip вҖ” the parts that trip you up in interviews. The parts senior developers actually understand.
 
```plaintext
The 6 books рҹ“ҡ
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
1. Get Started
2. Scope & Closures  вҶҗ most important рҹҺҜ
3. this & Object Prototypes
4. Types & Grammar
5. Async & Performance
6. ES6 & Beyond
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
```
 
**Why "Scope & Closures" matters for interviews:** рҹҺҜ
 
```javascript
// This question comes up in EVERY interview рҹӨ”
function outer() {
  let count = 0
 
  return function inner() {
    count++
    return count
  }
}
 
const counter = outer()
console.log(counter()) // 1
console.log(counter()) // 2
console.log(counter()) // 3
 
// Why does count keep incrementing?
// YDKJS Scope & Closures explains this perfectly рҹ§ 
```
 
> рҹ’Ў **How to use it:** Read "Scope & Closures" before any interview. Then "this & Object Prototypes." These two cover 70% of JavaScript interview questions about language fundamentals. рҹҺҜ
 
---
 
## вӯҗ Repo #4: free-programming-books
### рҹ”— github.com/EbookFoundation/free-programming-books
 
**Stars:** 335,000+ вӯҗ (most starred repo on ALL of GitHub! рҹӨҜ)
**What it is:** The world's biggest list of free programming books рҹ“ҡ
 
335,000 stars. That's not an accident. This repo is a treasure chest. рҹ’Һ
 
Thousands of free programming books, courses, and resources вҖ” organized by language, topic, and skill level.
 
```plaintext
What's in there for web devs рҹҢҗ
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
JavaScript:   50+ free books
React:        20+ free resources
Node.js:      15+ free books
TypeScript:   10+ free resources
CSS:          20+ free books
System Design:15+ free resources
 
Languages available: 30+
Total resources: 3,000+
Cost: вӮ№0 рҹҶ“
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
```
 
> рҹ’Ў **How to use it:** Search for whatever you're learning right now. There's almost certainly a free, high-quality book about it in here. Before buying any course вҖ” check this repo first. рҹҺҜ
 
---
 
## вӯҗ Repo #5: 30-seconds-of-code
### рҹ”— github.com/30-seconds/30-seconds-of-code
 
**Stars:** 120,000+ вӯҗ
**What it is:** Short, practical code snippets for everything рҹ’Ў
 
This repo is a collection of short JavaScript snippets that solve common problems вҖ” each one explained clearly and concisely.
 
```javascript
// Example snippets from the repo рҹҺҜ
 
// Debounce a function
const debounce = (fn, ms = 300) => {
  let timeoutId
  return function(...args) {
    clearTimeout(timeoutId)
    timeoutId = setTimeout(() => fn.apply(this, args), ms)
  }
}
 
// Deep clone an object
const deepClone = obj => JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(obj))
 
// Get unique values from array
const uniqueArray = arr => [...new Set(arr)]
 
// Check if date is weekend
const isWeekend = date =>
  [0, 6].includes(new Date(date).getDay())
 
// Capitalize first letter of each word
const titleCase = str =>
  str.replace(/\b\w/g, char => char.toUpperCase())
```
 
**Why this matters for interviews:** рҹҺҜ
 
These patterns come up constantly. Debounce, deep clone, array manipulation вҖ” interviewers love asking these. Having them internalized (not memorized вҖ” internalized) makes you look sharp. рҹ’Ә
 
> рҹ’Ў **How to use it:** Browse for 15 minutes daily. When you see a snippet you don't fully understand вҖ” copy it into your editor and break it down line by line. This is how senior developers think. рҹ§ 
 
---
 
## вӯҗ Repo #6: system-design-primer
### рҹ”— github.com/donnemartin/system-design-primer
 
**Stars:** 270,000+ вӯҗ
**What it is:** Complete system design interview prep вҖ” free рҹҸ—пёҸ
 
*"System design is for senior devs!"*
 
Not anymore. рҹҷ…
 
Companies at every level are asking basic system design questions to freshers in 2026. This repo prepares you for all of it вҖ” from basic concepts to complex distributed systems.
 
```plaintext
What's covered рҹҸ—пёҸ
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
Fundamentals (freshers need this):
  вң… How DNS works
  вң… Client-server model
  вң… Load balancers
  вң… Caching basics
  вң… Databases вҖ” SQL vs NoSQL
  вң… REST APIs
 
Intermediate (good to know):
  вң… CDNs
  вң… Message queues
  вң… Microservices basics
 
Advanced (for senior roles):
  вң… Designing Twitter/Instagram at scale
  вң… Distributed systems
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
```
 
> рҹ’Ў **How to use it:** Read the "Fundamentals" section only for fresher interviews. Don't get lost in the advanced content. Knowing how DNS works, what caching is, and why databases have indexes = 80% of what freshers get asked. рҹҺҜ
 
---
 
## вӯҗ Repo #7: build-your-own-x
### рҹ”— github.com/codecrafters-io/build-your-own-x
 
**Stars:** 300,000+ вӯҗ
**What it is:** Learn by building real things from scratch рҹ”Ё
 
This repo is pure gold for anyone who learns by doing. рҹ’Һ
 
It collects tutorials for building your own version of real technologies вҖ” databases, web servers, Git, browsers, and more. When you build something from scratch, you understand it at a completely different level.
 
```plaintext
Things you can build from scratch рҹ”Ё
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вң… Your own React (understand how it works!)
вң… Your own Git
вң… Your own web server
вң… Your own database
вң… Your own CLI tool
вң… Your own compiler
вң… Your own blockchain (just for fun рҹҳ„)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
```
 
**Why "Build Your Own React" is incredible for interviews:** рҹҺҜ
 
```javascript
// When you build React from scratch you understand:
// вҶ’ Why useState causes re-renders
// вҶ’ How the virtual DOM diffing actually works
// вҶ’ Why keys in lists matter
// вҶ’ How useEffect cleanup works
 
// Interview question: "How does React's reconciliation work?"
// Most devs: "Uh... it updates the DOM efficiently?"
// You after build-your-own-x: Explain it perfectly рҹҺҜ
```
 
> рҹ’Ў **How to use it:** Pick ONE thing to build from scratch. "Build your own React" is the best one for frontend devs. It will change how you understand React forever. рҹҡҖ

*рҹ”– P.S. вҖ” "Build Your Own React" from build-your-own-x is the single best thing you can do to deeply understand React. Block a weekend. Do it. You'll never look at React the same way again.*
